source.title: BSEE Accident Investigation Report - Structural Damage to Pipe Racking System

event.top_event: Dropped objects from Pipe Racking System
actions_taken: Job was stopped immediately upon collision
event.summary: On October 24, 2015 at 23:15 hours, while drilling operations were underway on the Discoverer Americas in Walker Ridge Block 160, a collision occurred between a stand of drill pipe and the rig's Pipe Racking System (PRS). The Assistant Driller lifted a 'Tall Stand' of drill pipe without following proper procedures and without maintaining visual confirmation via camera. The pipe struck the PRS Tub, causing the PRS Support Retainer (79 lbs) and a Teflon Bearing (3 lbs) to fall approximately 144 feet to the drill floor. The incident resulted in property damage of $16,282.
recommendations: Ensure crew members involved in pipe transfer operations are competent and aware of all procedures
Improve supervision of critical lifting operations
Enhance pre-job safety meeting discussions to specifically address hazards of transferring 'Tall Stands' of pipe
Enforce use of camera verification before and during pipe transfer operations

threats.name: Collision between drill pipe and PRS during transfer
Failure to follow proper lifting procedure for Tall Stands
Loss of visual confirmation during pipe transfer
Inadequate hazard identification in pre-job safety meeting

consequences.name: Structural damage to Pipe Racking System
Potential personnel injury from falling equipment

controls.name: Written Risk Assessment (WRA)
controls.barrier_role: Administrative control to identify hazards before work begins

supporting_text: Although a Written Risk Assessment (WRA) was performed and a safety meeting held before the work began, the crew failed to discuss the hazards involved with transferring 'Tall Stands' of pipe.
Failure to follow the Written Risk Assessment / Procedure for transferring drill pipe.

controls.name: Pre-job safety meeting
controls.barrier_role: Administrative control to brief crew on hazards and procedures

supporting_text: At 18:00 hours, the day of the incident, the drill crew onboard the Discoverer Americas performed a shift change and held a pre-job safety meeting with the crew members who were working on the rig floor to discuss the upcoming operations.
Crew failed to discuss the hazards of transferring the 'Tall Stand' both during the pre-tour safety meeting or before the job began.

controls.name: Proper procedure for lifting Tall Stands
controls.barrier_role: Administrative control specifying minimum lift height and tilt angle
controls.lod_basis: Procedural requirement for safe pipe transfer

supporting_text: The procedure for picking up a 'Tall Stand' of pipe calls for the stand to be picked a minimum of 2 to 3 inches and then retracted and tilted 45 degrees before continuing to pick up.
Based on the AD's statement, he was focused solely on the instructions given to him by the Floorhand and not on his camera or following the proper procedures as he was making the lift.

controls.name: Camera verification of pipe clearance
controls.barrier_role: Engineering/administrative control to visually confirm clearance before lift
controls.lod_basis: Real-time visual monitoring of critical lift operation

supporting_text: The Driller reminded the AD to visually verify the clearance between the top of the stand of pipe and the PRS tub by using the rig's camera.
Assistant Driller did not have a clear view of the top of the drill pipe.
Based on the AD's statement, he was focused solely on the instructions given to him by the Floorhand and not on his camera or following the proper procedures as he was making the lift.

controls.name: Floorhand visual observation and communication
controls.barrier_role: Administrative/human control for ground-level hazard monitoring
controls.lod_basis: Real-time observation and communication during lift

supporting_text: The AD instructed one of the Floorhands to keep a close watch on the drill pipe as it was transferred from the PRS to well center.
Floorhand was unaware of the location of the top of the pipe but continued flagging the Assistant Driller to pick up anyway.

controls.name: Supervision and competency verification
controls.barrier_role: Administrative control to ensure crew competence and procedure compliance

supporting_text: Poor Supervision: Failure to ensure crew members involved in the job were competent in their tasks and aware of all procedures associated with that job.
The Driller stated in an interview that he assumed that the task of transferring the 'Tall Stand' of pipe would be discussed further, and in more detail, once the crew had gotten to that point.

controls.name: Automatic PRS lowering system
controls.barrier_role: Engineering control to prevent continued damage after collision

supporting_text: As the stand of pipe came into contact with the PRS Tub, a loud bang was heard on the drill floor and the PRS automatically lowered the pipe back to the drill floor.

controls.name: Driller verification of equipment compatibility
controls.barrier_role: Administrative control to verify elevator and pipe dimensions match

supporting_text: Upon returning to the Driller's console, the Driller asked the AD if he had confirmed the Inside Diameter of the elevators and the Outside Diameter of the drill pipe to ensure they were compatible. The AD had only confirmed the size of the elevators and had to return to the drill floor to get measurements on the pipe.

notes.rules: JSON output only. mentioned fields must be evidence-based. Use null for unknown values.
